ai engineer: Build production-grade AI agents that are reliable, scalable, and can be discovered and used by other systems. Want their agents to have a proper identity, endpoint, and way to get paid for compute.
ml engineer: Ship ML-powered tools and agents to production. Need distribution channels and monetization without building billing infrastructure from scratch.
founding engineer: Move fast - ship agents that work end-to-end including discovery, identity, and payments. Don't want to reinvent the wheel on protocol-level concerns.
cto: Make architectural decisions that give the team long-term leverage. Want agents to be composable, discoverable, and monetizable without vendor lock-in.
software engineer: Build useful tools and agents. Want their work to be found and used by others, ideally with some economic upside without building a full SaaS.
solutions architect: Design multi-agent systems for clients. Need a standard way to make agents interoperable across LangChain, CrewAI, n8n without custom glue code.
devrel: Show developers what's possible with the platform. Need working examples that are genuinely useful - not toy demos.
developer advocate: Build credibility with the developer community by shipping real, useful integrations and demos.
indie hacker: Ship fast, get paid, build in public. Hate billing infrastructure. Want to focus on the agent logic, not the payment rails.
revops engineer: Automate the GTM stack using AI. Build workflows that run without manual intervention. Need agents to be reliable and callable by other systems.
gtm engineer: Own the technical GTM stack. Build agentic workflows that score leads, enrich data, and trigger outreach. Want agents to be composable.
n8n: Build automated workflows using n8n. Want AI agent nodes that are production-ready and can be monetized or shared in the community template library.
automation engineer: Build workflows that eliminate manual work at scale. Want AI agent components that are reliable, have proper endpoints, and integrate cleanly.
